content:
Query: Create a boids simulation in plain html and javascript. Render hundreds of fast moving and colorful boids on a 2D canvas. Split each component into separate files. Verify each component after they are written. The main runnable interface should be in `index.html`. Save all components within only this directory: `output/boids-simulation/`.


Respond with exactly one ```repl``` code block.

reply:
```repl
# Multi-file boids simulation. We'll delegate one child per file to write the file bodies.
# CONTRACT: each child reads this contract and the "target" line and must write the target file
# under output/boids-simulation/<filename>. Files (all files must live inside that directory):
#
# 1) index.html
#    - Minimal runnable HTML page.
#    - Includes a fullscreen canvas <canvas id="canvas"></canvas>.
#    - Links style.css and loads scripts in this order: utils.js, boid.js, flock.js, main.js
#    - Contains a short header or overlay with FPS and boid count placeholders.
#    - The page must be runnable by opening in a browser.
#
# 2) style.css
#    - Styles for body, canvas (cover full window), and overlay text (FPS/count).
#
# 3) utils.js
#    - Export/define small helpers used by boid/flock/main:
#      - function limit(vec, max) { ... }  OR simple vector helpers
#      - function wrapPosition(pos, width, height)
#    - Keep code plain ES6, no modules (use global functions/constructors) so index.html can include via <script>.
#
# 4) boid.js
#    - Define class Boid with:
#        constructor(x, y, id)
#        update(boids)
#        applyBehaviors(boids)
#        edges(width, height)
#        draw(ctx)
#      - Use vector operations (as plain objects {x,y}) and the utils above.
#      - Boids should draw as small triangles rotated in the velocity direction.
#      - Assign each boid a colorful hue (e.g., based on id or velocity).
#
# 5) flock.js
#    - Define class Flock with:
#        constructor(width, height, num)
#        step() or run(ctx)
#        addBoid(b)
#      - Manage an array of Boid instances, update them each frame, and draw them.
#
# 6) main.js
#    - Entry point that:
#        - Grabs canvas, resizes to window, creates a Flock with hundreds of boids (configurable, e.g., 300).
#        - Animates with requestAnimationFrame, clearing canvas and calling flock.run/draw.
#        - Updates overlay with FPS and boid count.
#        - Handles window resize.
#
# Style guidelines:
# - Plain JS (no bundlers, no modules) so files can be simply included.
# - Keep filenames EXACT: index.html, style.css, utils.js, boid.js, flock.js, main.js
# - All files written under output/boids-simulation/
#
# Each child should write the target file and return a short confirmation like "WROTE <path>".
#
contract = '''FILES:
- index.html: HTML entry. Include <canvas id="canvas"></canvas>, link style.css, include scripts in order: utils.js, boid.js, flock.js, main.js. Include overlay elements for FPS and count.
- style.css: Styles for body, canvas (fullscreen), overlay.
- utils.js: Plain JS helpers: vec operations (create, add, sub, mult, div, mag, setMag, limit), wrapPosition.
- boid.js: class Boid with update/applyBehaviors/edges/draw. Colorful hues per boid.
- flock.js: class Flock managing array of Boid, updating and drawing.
- main.js: Setup canvas, create Flock with ~300 boids, animation loop, FPS & count overlay, window resize handler.

Write the single target file path as output/boids-simulation/<filename>.
Do not write any other files.
'''

# Delegate one child per file
handles = [
    delegate("index_html", "Write the index.html file per the CONTRACT. Return exactly 'WROTE <path>' on success.", contract + "\n\ntarget: index.html"),
    delegate("style_css", "Write the style.css file per the CONTRACT. Return exactly 'WROTE <path>' on success.", contract + "\n\ntarget: style.css"),
    delegate("utils_js", "Write the utils.js file per the CONTRACT. Return exactly 'WROTE <path>' on success.", contract + "\n\ntarget: utils.js"),
    delegate("boid_js", "Write the boid.js file per the CONTRACT. Return exactly 'WROTE <path>' on success.", contract + "\n\ntarget: boid.js"),
    delegate("flock_js", "Write the flock.js file per the CONTRACT. Return exactly 'WROTE <path>' on success.", contract + "\n\ntarget: flock.js"),
    delegate("main_js", "Write the main.js file per the CONTRACT. Return exactly 'WROTE <path>' on success.", contract + "\n\ntarget: main.js"),
]
results = yield wait(*handles)

# Resumed: verify files were written and do lightweight content checks.
from os import path

base = "output/boids-simulation"
expected = {
    "index.html": ["<canvas", "script src=\"utils.js\"", "script src=\"boid.js\"", "script src=\"flock.js\"", "script src=\"main.js\"", "style.css"],
    "style.css": ["canvas", "body"],
    "utils.js": ["function", "limit(", "wrapPosition", "mag("],
    "boid.js": ["class Boid", "update(", "applyBehaviors", "draw(", "edges("],
    "flock.js": ["class Flock", "this.boids", "addBoid", "run(", "update("],
    "main.js": ["requestAnimationFrame", "new Flock", "canvas", "fps", "window.addEventListener"],
}

# Read files and assert basic invariants.
written = {}
for name in expected:
    p = path.join(base, name)
    try:
        src = read_file(p)
    except Exception as e:
        raise AssertionError(f"Missing expected file: {p}") from e
    written[name] = src
    # simple presence checks
    for token in expected[name]:
        if token not in src:
            raise AssertionError(f"Verification failed for {name}: missing token {token!r}")

# Extra sanity: index.html must include a canvas with id="canvas"
if 'id="canvas"' not in written["index.html"] and "id=canvas" not in written["index.html"]:
    raise AssertionError("index.html does not contain canvas with id=\"canvas\"")

# Sanity: main.js should set number of boids (numBoids or similar)
if "numBoids" not in written["main.js"] and "300" not in written["main.js"]:
    # okay if they used another variable name but check for a number literal 200-800; simple heuristic:
    if not any(str(n) in written["main.js"] for n in range(100, 801, 50)):
        # not fatal but warn via assertion to ensure interactive simulation
        raise AssertionError("main.js does not appear to configure a boid count (numBoids) between 100 and 800")

# All checks passed
done("Wrote and verified files in output/boids-simulation/: " + ", ".join(sorted(list(expected.keys()))))
```

}
